title: Bank of America AI and Digital Innovations Drive Client Engagement
text: Bank of America reported that AI and digital banking tools helped drive about 30 billion client interactions in the prior year, including digital logins and proactive alerts. Digital logins reached 16.6 billion, and alerts reached 13.3 billion. The bank said 81% of consumer and small business households, 86% of wealth management clients, and 86% of global banking clients were digitally engaged. Erica, the bank's AI-powered virtual financial assistant, reached more than 3.2 billion total interactions since launch, with 20.6 million users interacting with Erica nearly 700 million times in the prior year. Zelle adoption reached 25 million active users, with users sending and receiving 1.8 billion transactions worth $556 billion in 2025. This news supports Bank of America's investment story because digital engagement can deepen customer relationships, improve convenience, support payments growth, and create operational efficiencies.
